issue_title: Fails to serialize dictionaries that have numerical keys
issue_body:
The toml library fails to serialize dictionaries that have numerical keys. 

**Expected behavior:** Numerical keys in the dictionary to serialize will be converted to strings when `toml.dumps` or `toml.dump` is called. (Like the behavior of the equivalent json functions `json.dumps` and `json.dump`).

**Actual behavior:** `toml.dumps` and `toml.dump` both return a `TypeError`.

**Example** (toml version 0.9.2, python 2.7.6):
```
import toml
d = {1: "stuff"}
toml.dumps(d)
```

**Expected output:**
```
>>> import toml
>>> toml.dumps(d)
'{"1": "stuff"}'
```

**Actual Output:**
```
>>> import toml
>>> d = {1: "stuff"}
>>> toml.dumps(d)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/home/jakory/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/toml.py", line 597, in dumps
    addtoretval, sections = _dump_sections(o, "")
  File "/home/jakory/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/toml.py", line 619, in _dump_sections
    if not re.match(r'^[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$', section):
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/re.py", line 137, in match
    return _compile(pattern, flags).match(string)
TypeError: expected string or buffer
```

issue_title: Notify when refresh is triggered in security
issue_body:
- ~~Emit a signal when the refresh() is triggered (meaning access_token is expired) (we'll probably need a default handler)~~
- ~~Allow the user to override the called method when the signal is emitted.~~

Use the [Observer pattern](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Observer_pattern) to do this, as it's the easiest way to achieve this action without having headaches or using tons of libs. 
We'll need to have a way to add (append) observers to the security object, and trigger a "notify" action.

As we want the observer to have a specific method and arguments (as we know what he'll have to be notified), a "BaseSecurityObserver" class is "required".

```python

issue_title: Implement new user permission system based on groups
issue_body:
Django already has a group and a permission system, reuse these rather than our custom solution.

* Namespaces correspond to either user names or group names
* Users can be part of groups (multiple)
* Users can have ``groupread`` and/or ``groupwrite`` permissions, allowing them to read/write to the group namespace or to the user namespaces of other members in the group. By default these are not assigned yet.
* ``allowcopy`` and ``allowdelete`` permissions will be used for file management (issue #54)
* A new configuration setting ``creategroupnamespaces`` will automatically instruct the backend to create the group directories, provided the user also has write access

(Replaces issue #57)

issue_title: Native support for complex queries
issue_body:
*Originally reported by @MasterOdin in #11*

I would argue that it would be important for get_etexts to not only support one feature/value pair but potentially multiple. 

A potential use case that illustrates this would be how would I get only the german books by the author "Various".

The solution as proposed by your API would be:
```python
texts = get_etexts('author','various')
final_list = []
for text in texts:
    if get_metadata('language', text) != 'german':
        pass
    final_list.append(text)
```

which is somewhat weird as I'd kind of like the API to handle this internally (especially if I want to get even more specific with criteria and don't want to build up that if statement!)

So I'd say maybe change get_etexts to support passing in either two strings for one feature, or probably easier, a dictionary which would allow for any number of arguments:
```python
texts = get_etexts({'author':'various','language':'german'})
```

issue_title: Confusing cmake message when compiler does not support `thread_local`
issue_body:
When the compiler doesn't support `thread_local`, as on OSX with XCode < 8.0, running cmake reports that CUDA isn't being used with the following messages:

```
-- Found CUDA: /usr/local/cuda (found suitable version "7.5", minimum required is "7.5")
-- *** cuDNN V5.0 OR GREATER NOT FOUND.  DLIB WILL NOT USE CUDA. ***
-- *** If you have cuDNN then set CMAKE_PREFIX_PATH to include cuDNN's folder.
-- *** Dlib CUDA support requires C++11 but your compiler doesn't support it. ***
```

I do in fact have cuDNN installed, and the actual issue is that the test build failed due to my compiler not supporting `thread_local`, while it does support all the other necessary C++11 features (making the C++11 support error very confusing too). It took me a long time to get to the bottom of this, so I would suggest making the warning friendlier, e.g. by indicating that `thread_local` support is the likely problem.

issue_title: Investigate "directional" Taylor series
issue_body:
Due to the special property of the zero function used when intersecting curves

```
F(s, t) = B1(s) - B2(t)
```

all mixed derivatives are zero. This means the Taylor expansion is "isolated" to each function. Moving along a direction `ds = ah, dt = bh` the Taylor expansion becomes

```
  F(s + ah, t + bh)
= F(s, t)
+ h     [B1'(s)  | -B2'(t) ] [a  ]
                             [b  ]
+ h^2/2 [B1''(s) | -B2''(t)] [a^2]
                             [b^2]
+ ...
```

In particular, if the Jacobian `DF = [B1'(s) | -B2'(t)]` is singular at a point where `F(s, t) = [0, 0]^T`, then we can find at least one vector `[a, b]^T` in the null space of `DF` and the Taylor has the `h^2` term as its first nonzero term.

----

I'd like to try to generalize an approach from 1D Newton's method where if a double root is detected, then the iteration can switch to

```
x{n+1} = x{n} - f'(x{n}) / f''(x{n})
```

and higher derivatives can be used for roots of higher multiplicity.

(A root of multiplicity `m` can be detected if the method is in a region of convergence, i.e. `||x{n+1} - x{n}|| / ||x{n}||` remains small, but the ratio of consecutive errors `||x{n+2} - x{n+1}|| / ||x{n+1} - x{n}||` resembles `1 - 1/m`. This is even true if `m` is not an integer, e.g. when `f(x) = (x - 1)^{4/3}`.)

issue_title: Long line breaking with operators
issue_body:
When there is a long line, autopep8 breaks it like this:
```
1 = (-sp.sqrt(15.0) / 180.0) * self.K + \
            (1.0 / 18.0) * self.L
```
However, PEP 8 suggests breaking it before the operator to get a more readable code:
```
1 = (-sp.sqrt(15.0) / 180.0) * self.K \
       + (1.0 / 18.0) * self.L
```
Maybe, this can be improved?

issue_title: call os.fsync() following flush()
issue_body:
In tinydb/storages.py, line 112, a call to `self._handle.flush()` is made, in the `write()` method inside the class `JSONStorage`:

```
    def write(self, data):
        self._handle.seek(0)
        serialized = json.dumps(data, **self.kwargs)
        self._handle.write(serialized)
        self._handle.flush()
        self._handle.truncate()
```

Is the goal of this call to `flush` to ensure all internal buffers associated with `self._handle` are written to disk?

If so, it may be a good idea to make a call to `os.fsync()` to ensure this behavior.
ref: [Python Docs](https://docs.python.org/3/library/os.html#os.fsync)

I have already implemented this change in my fork.  If this is the case, I will create a PR.

issue_title: pycparser 2.14 wheel on PyPI differs from source distribution
issue_body:
Sometime in the past few hours, fresh virtualenvs using pycparser 2.14 started barfing in CFFI parsing with `AssertionError: sorry, but this version only supports 100 named groups`. Here is an example stack:

```
py27 runtests: commands[0] | python setup.py clean develop bdist_wheel
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "setup.py", line 52, in <module>
    import make_cffi
  File "/home/travis/build/indygreg/python-zstandard/make_cffi.py", line 105, in <module>
    ffi.cdef(source.decode('latin1'))
  File "/home/travis/build/indygreg/python-zstandard/.tox/py27/lib/python2.7/site-packages/cffi/api.py", line 105, in cdef
    self._cdef(csource, override=override, packed=packed)
  File "/home/travis/build/indygreg/python-zstandard/.tox/py27/lib/python2.7/site-packages/cffi/api.py", line 119, in _cdef
    self._parser.parse(csource, override=override, **options)
  File "/home/travis/build/indygreg/python-zstandard/.tox/py27/lib/python2.7/site-packages/cffi/cparser.py", line 299, in parse
    self._internal_parse(csource)
  File "/home/travis/build/indygreg/python-zstandard/.tox/py27/lib/python2.7/site-packages/cffi/cparser.py", line 304, in _internal_parse
    ast, macros, csource = self._parse(csource)
  File "/home/travis/build/indygreg/python-zstandard/.tox/py27/lib/python2.7/site-packages/cffi/cparser.py", line 260, in _parse
    ast = _get_parser().parse(csource)
  File "/home/travis/build/indygreg/python-zstandard/.tox/py27/lib/python2.7/site-packages/cffi/cparser.py", line 40, in _get_parser
    _parser_cache = pycparser.CParser()
  File "/home/travis/build/indygreg/python-zstandard/.tox/py27/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pycparser/c_parser.py", line 87, in __init__
    outputdir=taboutputdir)
  File "/home/travis/build/indygreg/python-zstandard/.tox/py27/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pycparser/c_lexer.py", line 66, in build
    self.lexer = lex.lex(object=self, **kwargs)
  File "/home/travis/build/indygreg/python-zstandard/.tox/py27/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pycparser/ply/lex.py", line 911, in lex
    lexobj.readtab(lextab, ldict)
  File "/home/travis/build/indygreg/python-zstandard/.tox/py27/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pycparser/ply/lex.py", line 233, in readtab
    titem.append((re.compile(pat, lextab._lexreflags | re.VERBOSE), _names_to_funcs(func_name, fdict)))
  File "/home/travis/build/indygreg/python-zstandard/.tox/py27/lib/python2.7/re.py", line 194, in compile
    return _compile(pattern, flags)
  File "/home/travis/build/indygreg/python-zstandard/.tox/py27/lib/python2.7/re.py", line 249, in _compile
    p = sre_compile.compile(pattern, flags)
  File "/home/travis/build/indygreg/python-zstandard/.tox/py27/lib/python2.7/sre_compile.py", line 583, in compile
    "sorry, but this version only supports 100 named groups"
AssertionError: sorry, but this version only supports 100 named groups
```

This CI environment is pinning versions, so it should be deterministic.

Strangely, I was able to reproduce this error on a fresh virtualenv but not an existing one. I diffed the contents of the virtualenvs and saw that a number of `.py` files related to pycparser had changed!

Looking at https://pypi.python.org/pypi/pycparser, there is a `pycparser-2.14.tar.gz` uploaded  	2015-06-10. There is also a `pycparser-2.14-py2.py3-none-any.whl` uploaded today.

If you compare the source in the .tar.gz from the wheel, it varies.

It looks like the wheel just uploaded to PyPI was cut from a different commit than what the tar.gz was cut from. And since modern versions of pip prefer downloading wheels over the source distribution, pip is downloading the wheel.

Please delete the bad wheel from PyPI. Please also consider cutting a 2.15 release that is the same commit as 2.14 but with matching sources.

issue_title: XEP-0092: Correctly handle requirement of name and version in result
issue_body:
@sebastianriese correctly pointed out that XEP-0092 REQUIRES that ``<name/>`` and ``<version/>`` are included in a result.

We have three options:

* Force ``<name/>`` and ``<version/>`` to be the defaults (``"aioxmpp"``/``aioxmpp.__version__``) instead of None when deleted.
* Do not answer queries if either is set to None (reply with ``<service-unavailable/>``)
* Set them to ``"unspecified"``, ``"unspecified"`` instead.

Opinions?

issue_title: Version 11.0 compliant with aiohttp2
issue_body:
The title explains itself ...

Another major change is the deprecation of `message_loop()`. I have long felt uneasy about the mess in that method, the lumping together of two ways to obtain updates - the `getUpdates` loop and the source queue used by webhook. 

Those logic have been moved into module `telepot.loop` and `telepot.aio.loop`, with a better structure. The classes are:

- `MessageLoop` - to obtain updates with a `getUpdates` loop
- `OrderedWebhook` and `Webhook` - to be fed data by the web application

Usages are detailed in the [intro](http://telepot.readthedocs.io), [reference](http://telepot.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference.html), and [examples](https://github.com/nickoala/telepot/tree/master/examples).

`message_loop()` will still be there for some time. But new applications should definitely use the `loop` module.

Sorry for the inconvenience. Thanks for using telepot :smile:

issue_title: Invalid no-ASCII unicode string handling
issue_body:
Migrated from https://code.google.com/p/colorama/issues/detail?id=21
Reported by av1024, Feb 23, 2011

What steps will reproduce the problem?
1. import and initalize colorama with deafults
2. print u"Some non-ASCII text ТЕСТ Русского"

What is the expected output?
Some non-ASCII text ТЕСТ Русского

What do you see instead?
UnicodeEncodeError: 'ascii' codec can't encode characters in position 0-5: ordinal not in range(128)

What version of the product are you using? On what operating system?
Python 2.7 (x32)
Windows 7 x64 Untimate (with Eng/Rus locales)

Please provide any additional information below.
Looks like wrapped write method hoes not inherit/use original stdout encoding. Possible fixes are (two ways):

A. use sys.setdefaultencoding(Your-Console-OEM-Encoding) # Wrong way IMHO. I don't know the simple method to determine right console mode (ANSI/OEM) and OEM encoding except reading 'stdout.encoding' property

B. Patch ansitowin32 to force-encode unicode output before .write:

--- D:\lg\py\colorama-0.1.18\colorama\ansitowin32.py    Tue May 18 14:43:54 2010
+++ ansitowin32.py  Wed Feb 23 19:10:40 2011
@@ -144,7 +144,10 @@
 
     def write_plain_text(self, text, start, end):
         if start < end:
-            self.wrapped.write(text[start:end])
+            if isinstance(text, unicode):
+                self.wrapped.write(text[start:end].encode(self.wrapped.encoding))
+            else:
+                self.wrapped.write(text[start:end])
             self.wrapped.flush()

issue_title: Still doesn't work with Python 3.6
issue_body:
https://github.com/tiran/defusedxml/commit/1d342237b560e29e8401d0a22a776b52b09e0ae2 is not sufficient to make defusedxml work properly with Python 3.6. It stops defusedxml outright choking in `_get_py3_cls`, but it doesn't *work* properly. It results in `common._generate_etree_functions()` failing, as can be seen in the 10 failed tests from the Travis run for that commit.

I naively attempted just changing the `if PY26 or PY31:` condition in `_generate_etree_functions()` to `if PY26 or PY31 or sys.version_info >= (3, 6):` , but that's not good enough either because that code really is tailored to the Python 2.6 / 3.1 implementation; it doesn't work at all for the Python 3.6 implementation. It seems like we need an entirely new approach to defusing iterparse for 3.6, but I'm not smart enough to write that. At least, not at this time in the morning...

This is a bit of a problem for us (Fedora) as Python 3.6 landed in Rawhide today, and now defusedxml won't install and can't be rebuilt (unless we ignore the fact that it's broken and disable the tests...), and several things that depend on it can't install or be rebuilt either.

issue_title: Retrieve images
issue_body:
From a quick code and documentation search, there's no functionality for handling images sent, or sending images. Would this be hard to implement? There doesn't even seem to be functionality for downloading raw image data as a string. At least this functionality would be helpful, because:

```python
import discord
from PIL import Image
from StringIO import StringIO

client = discord.Client()
client.login('user','password')

def on_message(message):
    buff = StringIO()
    buff.write(message.content)
    try:
        im = Image.open(buff)
        isImage=True
    except:
        text = message.content
        isImage=False
    if isImage:
        im.save("img.jpg")
    else:
        print text
```
Setting it up like this would be an easy way to download images. Uploading images might work similarly.

issue_title: Method `page.sections` return html stuff in some cases
issue_body:
Hello,
I'm using this library to get textual descriptions for classes in the CUB 2011 dataset.

For each class of the 200 bird classes in the CUB dataset, I get the relative wikipedia page and look at the sections with the property `page.sections`.
In some cases I get html codes inside the sections, for example:

```
from mediawiki import MediaWiki
wikipedia = MediaWiki()
page = wikipedia.page('Pied billed Grebe')
print(page.sections)
```
output:
`[u'Taxonomy and name', u'Subspecies<sup>[8]</sup>', u'Description', u'Vocalization', u'Distribution and habitat', u'Behaviour', u'Breeding', u'Diet', u'Threats', u'In culture', u'Status', u'References', u'External links']`

Then, if I use the `page.section(str)` method with the string `u'Subspecies<sup>[8]</sup>'`:
```
print(page.section(page.sections[1]))
```
output: ```None```

The correct string to find the object with the method `page.sections(str)` is simply `'Subspecies'`.

I actually managed to fix this issue implementing this method:

```
def fixed_sections(page_content, verbose=False):
    sections = []
    import re
    section_regexp = r'\n==* .* ==*\n' # '== {STUFF_NOT_\n} =='
    found_obj = re.findall( section_regexp, page.content)
    
    if found_obj is not None:
        for obj in found_obj:
            obj = obj.lstrip('\n= ').rstrip(' =\n')
            sections.append(obj)
            if verbose: print("Found section: {}".format(obj))
    return sections

correct_sections  = fixed_sections(page.content)
print(correct_sections)
print(page.section(correct_sections[1]))
```

With this code I get the correct output, i.e. the content of the section (sub-section in this case):

```
[u'Taxonomy and name', u'Subspecies', u'Description', u'Vocalization', u'Distribution and habitat', u'Behaviour', u'Breeding', u'Diet', u'Threats', u'In culture', u'Status', u'References', u'External links']
P. p. podiceps, (Linnaeus, 1758), North America to Panama & Cuba.
P. p. antillarum, (Bangs, 1913), Greater & Lesser Antilles.
P. p. antarcticus, (Lesson, 1842), South America to central Chile & Argentina.
```


This fix it works for me, but it require to execute a reg-exp for each page, so maybe is not optimal.

issue_title: KafkaClient in client_async.py fails in python3
issue_body:
I'm running python 3.4.2 with Kafka 0.8.1.1

I've had a quick look and submitted a (broken) PR.

Issue looks to be that ConnectionError isn't defined in PY3:

At line 23, `six.PY2 == True` defines `ConnectionError = None` but six.PY3 will not assign it to anything. The assertion checking for the exception type at line 628 will then fail.

The follow snippet demonstrates this under 3.4.2:

```
>>> import six
>>> if six.PY2:
...     ConnectionError = None
... 
>>> f = IOError
>>> assert isinstance(f, ConnectionError)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
AssertionError
```

Is it correct code?
```python
from kafka import KafkaProducer
producer = None
def handle_message(msg):
    if producer is None:
        producer = KafkaProducer(bootstrap_servers='localhost:1234')

issue_title: mlogvis: Add nscanned/n as option for y-axis
feature/bug:  add_picture/from_file probably should not seek(0)
issue_body:
image.py: 169 has this
            # assume image_file is a file-like object                                    
            image_file.seek(0)                                                          
            blob = image_file.read()
            filename = None

The problem is not all file-like objects support seek() (as documented in the main python docs).   In particular, URL readers that present file-like objects error out on seek():
    r = urllib2.urlopen("http://whatever")
    pic = shapes.add_picture(r, 0, 0)

Is there a strong reason to seek(0)?   Seems like the caller should be responsible for positioning the stream to be read() by from_file, no?

issue_title: Issue writing empty (but formatted) cells
issue_body:
Hi,

I am using Python version 3.6.4 and XlsxWriter 1.0.4; the issue happens when opening the generated xlsx with Excel version 2016 (Windows10) or 2013 (Windows7).
Tried running the code both with Pycharm and the Python IDLE, nothing changes (makes sense).
Tried running the code on different devices too, all the same.

I am using XlsxWriter to create a xlsx file from a list of dictionaries (1 dict becames one row).
Everything works fine till I try to write an empty but formatted cell: if that cell has any kind of format I get a visual glitch once I open it in Excel.

Basically I open it in Excel and as soon I move the sheet (it's like 150 columns) some of the cells that should be empty show some garbled characters on them, characters that I recognize as part of other cells content; if I click on the cell the cell is empty, like it should be. It's like there's an overlay of characters that appear or disappear as soon I move the sheet right and left or zoom it, just to reappear later.

I used:

worksheet.write(i1, i2, '', frmt)          
worksheet.write(i1, i2, None, frmt)
worksheet.write_blank(i1, i2, None, frmt)

All of them gives me the glitch. Using:

worksheet.write(i1, i2, '')   

There's no more visual glitch, but I lose the format.

I need to know if there are any known issues with writing big xlsx files with a lot of blank formatted cells.

Here is a cutdown of the full code, I hope it can help to understand what I'm doing. 

```
import xlsxwriter

data_dict_list = [{'key1': 1, 'key2': 'gl', 'key3': True}, {'key1': 42, 'key2': 'hfds', 'key3': False}, ]
data_key_list = 'key1', 'key2', 'key3'
cell_formats = {'align': 'left'}, {'align': 'left', 'bold': True}, {'align': 'center'}


class XlsxStuff:

    def __init__(self):
        self.workbook = None

    def xlsx_open(self, xlsx_path):
        self.workbook = xlsxwriter.Workbook(xlsx_path)

    def sheet_write(self, sheet_name, data_set, key_list, formats):

        worksheet = self.workbook.add_worksheet(sheet_name)

        for i1 in range(len(data_set)):
            for i2 in range(len(key_list)):
                frmt = self.workbook.add_format(formats[i2])
                if key_list[i2] in data_set[i1] and (data_set[i1])[key_list[i2]] is not None:
                    worksheet.write(i1, i2, (data_set[i1])[key_list[i2]], frmt)
                else:
                    worksheet.write_blank(i1, i2, None, frmt)

    def xlsx_close(self):
        self.workbook.close()


excel = XlsxStuff()	
excel.xlsx_open('excel_file.xlsx')
excel.sheet_write('DataSet1', data_dict_list, data_key_list, cell_formats)
excel.xlsx_close()
```

I'd like to point out that this issue happened to me in the past too, when I used a simpler 1 sheet output without the need of a class; back then I didn't need to format the empty cells so I just didn't write them as a workaround.

The actual data set is around 400+ dicts with 150 key/value pair each (so 400+ rows * 150 colums); I can't share the data unfortunately.

I don't understand what is going wrong, xlsxwriter never had any issue before this one; I don't think I'm doing it wrong but if I am I'll be grateful if I can be helped.

Thanks a lot. any help will be appreciated. 

Oh, and thak you for xlsxwriter, it's really a neat xlsx file writer (like: it works like I want it to XD)

issue_title: Possible improvements to the speed performance benchmark
issue_body:
I've done some refactoring of the speed benchmark code so that you can pass in the number of sheets and I also have a driver script to run with different Python versions. I suspect you already know that xlsxwriter in standard mode can be quite a bit faster that constant memory when writing multiple sheets (of course as long as you've got the memory). 

As I'm not interested in the under-featured pyexcelerate I've removed it from my benchmarks which makes it less suitable for a PR so I'm providing the links to the snippets.

Benchmarking: https://bitbucket.org/snippets/charlie_x/5edaGE
Driver script: https://bitbucket.org/snippets/charlie_x/gebj7M

issue_title: Support Plex Webhooks?
issue_body:
I wanted to start a discussion on Webhooks.

Plex announced Alexa integration today. While exciting, I don't think that we have much impact from this.  However, in the article about Alexa, they mentioned now supporting Webhooks for PlexPass members.  This sounds interesting to me..

Is this useful for API users in any way? -- I don't know much about it, but I imagine adding a method to supply a callback function when we notice a webhook event could be very useful. It would allow things like custom desktop notifications or taking some other action on events. Our API would simply make this integration easier to hook into.

issue_title: Instance kerning data should reflect switched masters
issue_body:
A glyph can have different masters in specific instances. This way a glyph can have a radically different construction in some weights or widths. In Superpolator this is the **rules** feature.

Currently the kerning is unaware of these new masters. That means the kerning data generated for that glyph will be invalid as they depend on the old glyph geometry. So the kerning will need to be manipulated to insert the kerning of the replacement masters. 

Any number of glyphs may be switched, so it will probably need to be some sort of child element of the kerning element.

this is the migration:
```python
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
# Generated by Django 1.11.7 on 2017-11-10 11:47
from __future__ import unicode_literals

from django.db import migrations, models
import django.db.models.deletion


class Migration(migrations.Migration):

    dependencies = [
        ('django_ca', '0006_auto_20170505_1251'),
    ]

    operations = [
        migrations.AlterField(
            model_name='certificateauthority',
            name='parent',
            field=models.ForeignKey(blank=True, null=True, on_delete=django.db.models.deletion.SET_NULL, related_name='children', to='django_ca.CertificateAuthority'),
        ),
    ]
```

issue_title: docker-squash looses history information
issue_body:
I'm doing docker-squash relative to some base layer, then doing couple commits on the squashed image
and then trying to do the squash again relative to the same base layer..  docker-squash does not find the base layer in the latest image.  It seems that the reason is loss history info in squashed image.
This following bash scrip demoes the effect by showing the the Parent field in the squashed image manifest is empty.
`#!/bin/bash


 cat >Dockerfile <<EOF
 FROM alpine:3.5


RUN mkdir data && echo Begin >/data/file.txt

EOF


docker build -t squashtest:base . 

image=squashtest:base
for i in  1 2 ; do

	docker run --name=squashtest.$i.1 $image  sh -c "echo Line $i.1  >>data/file.txt"
	docker commit squashtest.$i.1  squashtest:$i.1
	docker run --name=squashtest.$i.2 squashtest:$i.1 sh -c "echo Line $i.2  >>data/file.txt"
	docker commit squashtest.$i.2 squashtest:$i.2
	image=squashtest:$i.2

done

docker inspect squashtest:squash-1.2 | grep -i Parent`

issue_title: ImportError: No module named loading with Django 1.10
issue_body:
Traceback:

````
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "./manage.py", line 24, in <module>
    execute_from_command_line(sys.argv)
  File "[... redacted ...]/django/core/management/__init__.py", line 367, in execute_from_command_line
    utility.execute()
  File "[... redacted ...]/django/core/management/__init__.py", line 341, in execute
    django.setup()
  File "[... redacted ...]/django/__init__.py", line 27, in setup
    apps.populate(settings.INSTALLED_APPS)
  File "[... redacted ...]/django/apps/registry.py", line 108, in populate
    app_config.import_models(all_models)
  File "[... redacted ...]/django/apps/config.py", line 199, in import_models
    self.models_module = import_module(models_module_name)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/importlib/__init__.py", line 37, in import_module
    __import__(name)
  File "[... redacted ...]/social_auth/models.py", line 6, in <module>
    from social_auth.utils import setting
  File "[... redacted ...]/social_auth/utils.py", line 14, in <module>
    from django.db.models.loading import get_model
ImportError: No module named loading
````

Apparently this was removed at the end of 2014: https://github.com/remohammadi/django/commit/4e65f195e1b10d83bb7edc38c908747c4fd537b8

A quick fix seems to be the following:

````python
try:
    from django.db.models.loading import get_model
except ImportError:
    from django.apps import apps
    get_model = apps.get_model
````

Also, there seem to be other errors related to importlib (see #841) which can be fixed as:

````python
try:
    from django.utils.importlib import import_module
except ImportError:
    import importlib
````

Generally it seems that django-social-auth is not well maintained and isn't compatible with Django 1.10

issue_title: Deprecate verify_ssl arguments
issue_body:
Disabling SSL verification is strongly discouraged and should not be this easy :-) Also, there are a huge number of connection customizations the user may want to do. `requests` suggests custom `HTTPAdapter` classes to handle this customization, and we support registering custom classes.

Let's remove the `verify_ssl` options in favour of:

```python
from requests.adapters import HTTPAdapter
from exchangelib.protocol import BaseProtocol

class NoVerifyHTTPAdapter(HTTPAdapter):
    def init_poolmanager(self, *args, **kwargs):
        kwargs['verify'] = False
        super(NoVerifyHTTPAdapter, self).init_poolmanager(*args, **kwargs)

BaseProtocol.HTTP_ADAPTER_CLS = NoVerifyHTTPAdapter
```

We can even provide `NoVerifyHTTPAdapter` for convenience.

issue_body:
Python3 installation on Windows [fails](https://ci.appveyor.com/project/jakubroztocil/httpie/build/1.0.135/job/9t6knidopp5qxwxd#L77):

```
Reading https://pypi.python.org/simple/pytest-httpbin/
Best match: pytest-httpbin 0.0.6.macosx-10.10-x86-64
Downloading https://pypi.python.org/packages/any/p/pytest-httpbin/pytest-httpbin-0.0.6.macosx-10.10-x86_64.tar.gz#md5=c29c3895fd016dccbdb9057e33602031
Processing pytest-httpbin-0.0.6.macosx-10.10-x86_64.tar.gz

Notice that `pip` tries to install the `pytest-httpbin-0.0.6.macosx-10.10-x86_64.tar.gz` package, which according to [PyPi](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/pytest-httpbin) is a '"dumb" binary built for Darwin-14.0.0'. I think it can/should be removed from PyPi.

(Btw, there has been a similar issue with HTTPie when I accidentally published a `bdist` package: https://github.com/jakubroztocil/httpie/issues/301)

issue_title: Exceptions for permissible HTTP situations
issue_body:
Currently, SSLify watches DEBUG to see if it should SSLify or not a request.  However, it would be useful to be able to provide a more advanced criteria for per-request SSLification.

For example, using SSLify on Amazon Elastic Beanstalk, SSLify keeps trying to redirect the health checker, which results in failing checks.  It would be nice to be able to add a "criteria" to the list of criteria in redirect_to_ssl, so that you could check to see if, for example, the user agent was the ELB Health Checker and the requested URL was the URL to be tested.  Or even, just to exclude a URL from the SSLification.

If the maintainer would be open to such a patch, I'll submit it.

issue_title: manual setting of next querystring in unauthorized_handler()
issue_body:
I have a need (want?) to be able to manually set the `next` parameter passed to `login_url()` in `unauthorized_handler()`.

In my flask app I want to redirect my users to specific pages after they log in depending on their roles, not the page they were trying to access.

Additionally, users log in with OAuth2, and when it fails in certain ways it redirects them back to the login page (desired behavior), but I do not want the next parameter to contain the authorization/authentication URL so in this case I do not want `next` to be set at all.

I believe all that is need to implement this is is to add an argument for `next` to the `unauthorized_handler()` function set to `request.url` by default, which is what is currently always passed to `login_url()`. That way the current behavior is maintained, but allows for manually setting the `next` argument that is passed to `login_url()`. This also supports passing `None` to `login_url()` to stop the `next` querystring showing on the login page at all.

Cheers
Richard

issue_body:
Perhaps this was an intentional design decision, but I've found no mention of it in continuum's documentation or existing issues. So here's at least a note for others who encounter this issue.

Continuum depends on the ORM events `after_delete`, `after_update`, and `after_insert` to make the corresponding version table inserts. (see https://github.com/kvesteri/sqlalchemy-continuum/blob/7089b813c0b31de52c5b30d797a450d16e5f33e0/sqlalchemy_continuum/manager.py#L135)

As documented by SQLAlchemy, none of these events are fired for bulk operations (http://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/latest/orm/persistence_techniques.html#orm-compatibility). As a result, continuum does not update version tables in response to bulk operations.

So statements of the form:

```Model.query[.filter_by(), etc].delete()```

Must be written in the less terse and efficient form:

```instances_to_delete = Model.query[.filter_by(), etc].all()
for instance in instances_to_delete:
    instance.delete()```

issue_title: Revert behaves weird
issue_body:
Suppose I have the following:

```python
article = Article()
article.name = u'Some article'
article.content = u'Some content'
session.add(article)
session.commit() # Creates 1 version of INSERT operation (version#0)
article.name = u'Any article'
session.commit() # Creates 1 version of UPDATE operation (version#1)
version = article.versions[0]
session.delete(article)
session.commit() # creates 1 version of DELETE operation (version#2)
```

- If I revert `version#0`, I understand that I want delete the model that was created
- If I revert `version#1`, I understand that I want to replace the model properties with the `version#1` properties
- If I revert `version#2`, I understand that I want to recreate the deleted model

but what [I've seen](https://github.com/kvesteri/sqlalchemy-continuum/blob/master/sqlalchemy_continuum/reverter.py#L111-L127) was that:

- If I revert `version#0`, the model is being created
- If I revert `version#1`, the model receives `version#1` properties (as expected)
- If I revert `version#2`, the model is being deleted

Does it seem right to you this type of behaviour or is it just me?

issue_body:

issue_title: Tip: How to use this pylint-commit-hook when the python code is not in git root.
issue_body:
## The case

You have your python code in a subdirectory with local includes from the git repository. Normally within `__init__.py` Without a local .pylintrc the local includes will hurt the result quite a lot. 

The Git repository:

```
<git root>
    sub_dir
         python_package
               __init__.py
               module1.py
               module2.py
```
## Solution

Add the file: `<git root>/.pylintrc` that have this data in it:

```
[MASTER]
init-hook="sys.path.insert(0,os.path.abspath('sub_dir'))"
```

Now pylint will have the right python path when it is running.

issue_title: Migration of fields with notnull=True is broken
issue_body:
When you migrate a field that will have the NOT NULL constraint after migration but does not have any default value, you'll get error message like this (example from PostgreSQL):
`ProgrammingError('ERROR', '23502', 'column "fieldname__tmp" contains null values')`

NOT NULL constraint obviously breaks the current ADD-UPDATE-DROP sequence because the ADD query will not specify any temporary default value. There are two possible solutions:
  * Create the column with temporary default value and remove it at the end of migration
  * Create the column without the NOT NULL constraint and add it at the end of migration

issue_title: Remove redundant exists() check for client create_database call
issue_body:
When using the client `create_database` method to create a database you are effectively making a call to the database `exists` method twice.  Once [here}(https://github.com/cloudant/python-cloudant/blob/master/src/cloudant/client.py#L227) in `create_database` and then one more time [here](https://github.com/cloudant/python-cloudant/blob/master/src/cloudant/database.py#L340) in the database `create` method.

I propose that we fix this logic and remove the redundant call to `exists` by adding a keyword argument to the `create` method in CouchDatabase that would allow for turning on/off the "exists" check [here](https://github.com/cloudant/python-cloudant/blob/master/src/cloudant/database.py#L340).  The default would be for the check to be "on" so that no current behavior is affected.

I then propose fixing the logic [here](https://github.com/cloudant/python-cloudant/blob/master/src/cloudant/client.py#L226-L232) to utilize this new switch to turn off the redundant exists check in the `create_database` method of the CouchDB client class.

issue_title: Cloudant exception should throw 412 when database already exists
issue_body:
Currently we are throwing a `CloudantClientException` with 409 status code when it should be 412: https://github.com/cloudant/python-cloudant/blob/master/src/cloudant/client.py#L229. 
This can be confirmed by trying to create an existing database and checking the status code for `resp`: https://github.com/cloudant/python-cloudant/blob/master/src/cloudant/database.py#L343.

issue_title: test_operator_gpu.test_pooling_versions has fixed seed that can mask flakiness
issue_body:
The unit test in title have been using fixed seed to mask flakiness. Suggested action:
1. Evaluate whether the test is flaky without fixed seed. If not, remove seed. Else move to 2
2. If test is flaky, determine whether it's an actual uncaught edge case. If so, fix the operator. Else move to 3
3. If numerical instability is inevitable, adjust tolerance level appropriately.

issue_title: batch padding bug in ml.dmlc.mxnet.spark.io.LabeledPointIter
issue_body:
if sample_size % batch_size != 0, the last batch generated by next() is still initialized with NDArray.empty() using original batch_size and set pad = (batch_size - real_size).

However, the mxnet training process seems to ignore the pad field of DataBatch. The whole batch is taken into calculation, which may produce NaN result in my linear regression model. I find that the uninitialized part of the last batch contains randomly huge numbers like xxxxxE10. 

Using NDArray.zeros() to initialize the batch or just discarding the last batch can fix in my case. And is it possible to use different batch_size for different batch in the DataIter object?

issue_title: prepare_mkl.sh script issue report
issue_body:
## Description
Build MXNet with Intel MKLML under Linux will link with incorrect MKL library instead of MKLML library due to a bug in prepare_mkl.sh.

The root cause is in prepare_mkl.sh USE_MKLML flag is wrongly set to 0 even when compiling with MKLML which make libmxnet.so linked with unexpected MKL library.

To be more accurate, there is issue in below find statement (in prepare_mkl.sh line 118) that would return empty even libmklml_gnu.so exists under MKLROOT directory.
`if [ -z find $MKLROOT -name libmklml_gnu.so -o -name libmklml.dylib -print -quit ] `

The fix should be something like below (like add bracket to embrace the "or" logic):
`diff --git a/prepare_mkl.sh b/prepare_mkl.sh`
`index 97a1e49..e048f59 100755`
`--- a/prepare_mkl.sh`
`+++ b/prepare_mkl.sh`
`@@ -115,7 +115,7 @@ if [ -z $MKLROOT ]; then`
`fi`

`\# Check what MKL lib we have in MKLROOT`
`-if [ -z find $MKLROOT -name libmklml_gnu.so -o -name libmklml.dylib -print -quit ]; then`
`+if [ -z find $MKLROOT \( -name libmklml_gnu.so -o -name libmklml.dylib \) -print -quit ]; then`
`USE_MKLML=0`
`elif [ -z find $MKLROOT -name libmkl_core.so -print -quit ]; then`
`USE_MKLML=1`

## Build info (Required if built from source)

Compiler: GCC
HW:  Intel CPU
MXNet commit hash:
ae0fe77c25c51f2afecb178c610cfcd0b8a18aac

Build command:
make -j 12 USE_OPENCV=1 USE_MKL2017=1 USE_MKL2017_EXPERIMENTAL=1 USE_BLAS=mkl USE_PROFILER=1 MKLML_ROOT=/home/jinhuang/downloads/mklml_lnx_2018.0.1.20171007

## What have you tried to solve it?
1.  Applied a fix in prepare_mkl.sh to fix this issue.
2.  Added a test script to check if MKLML is installed correctly (linked with expected MKLML library) after 
 build/install.
3.  After the fix,  MXNet with MKLML could be compiled successfully, and the image training performance is about 42.66 images/sec on skylake 8180 using bs64 (use train_imagenet.py script)


Other info:  similar issue is also reported in another thread https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/issues/8881

issue_title: Please add option for pygment to use dark background colors
issue_body:
Hey,

I'm using the SQL printing option of `shell_plus`, and at night when I code, flux removes the blue colors from my display. This results of the SQL commands displayed in dark blue, unreadable on a dark background, which I have in my Terminal.

Here's the culprit:

https://github.com/django-extensions/django-extensions/blob/ad2913ffe4c878723b9c490e6b83d4d9250abfbd/django_extensions/management/commands/shell_plus.py#L383-L387

If you could replace line 386 to `pygments.formatters.TerminalFormatter(bg='dark')`, or at least add a configurable value for having a dark background, that would be really appreciated. Now, I monkeypatched it by hand, so at least I can see the SQL results.

issue_title: SQS queue URL returned from sqs.Queue.url differs from the one in console
issue_body:
The SQS url returned by client is of format
https://{queue_region}.**queue**.amazonaws.com/{id}/{queue_name}

and the SQS url listed in the console is of the format:
https://**sqs**.{queue_region}.amazonaws.com/{id}/{queue_name}

This makes a difference when passing to a Mechanical Turk notification as a destination URL (the second format is accepted, the first is not).

Steps to reproduce: (using python3.6, boto3 version 1.7.62)
```
session = boto3.Session(<access_key>, <secret_key>)
sqs = session.resource('sqs', region_name=<queue_region>)
queue = sqs.get_queue_by_name(QueueName=<queue_name>)
print(queue.url)
```
The other URL can be found in the AWS console under SQS.
In case that matters, it was tested for region eu-west-1.

Which one of the formats is correct and why are they different?

issue_title: The API have some problems ？
_element should become part of the official API (allows Selenium calls)
issue_body:
Let's look at some code similar to something I write using Splinter:

```
a = browser.find_by_id("foo")
txt = a._element.get_attribute('outerHTML')
try:
   txt.find('some HTML')
except:
   print("Test FAIL")
   return -1
```

The point being, I'm using _element to access a method in the underlying Selenium that Splinter does not appear to support.

We should make this part of the official API: If you want to do something with an element and Splinter does not support it, use _element to make the required Selenium call. This way, already written code does not need to be revised.

issue_title: CLI design principle and strategy
issue_body:
We're developing a simple and easy to use CLI for the mapbox client module both as a service to customers and as a check on the usability of the module.

- The CLI should be 99% about arg/opt definition and interoperation with other programs in the shell.
- The CLI will have first class support for JSON and GeoJSON.
- We'll rely on other programs like sed, awk, jq for finer parsing of responses from APIs at first, absorbing some of this when it makes sense.

There is a name conflict with another critical `mapbox` program. I wish we'd reserved `mapbox` for customer-facing programs and libraries, but we've crossed that bridge. Let's think of a better name for the CLI than "mapbaux" soon.

issue_title: Is it a bug that consumer_timeout_ms doesn't work for BalancedConsumer?
issue_body:
I have set `consumer_timeout_ms` when I am getting a `BalancedConsumer` object.
```python
consumer = topic.get_balanced_consumer(
        'testgroup',
        consumer_timeout_ms=1000)
message = consumer.consume(block=True) # it blocks forever if there is no message
```

After some debugs, I found that `consumer_timeout_ms` works in the internal consumer. But 
it cann't exit the loop in the function `consume` of `BalancedConsumer`.
```python
def consume(self, block=True):
    """Get one message from the consumer

    :param block: Whether to block while waiting for a message
    :type block: bool
    """
    message = None
    while message is None:
        try:
            message = self._consumer.consume(block=block) 
        except ConsumerStoppedException:
            continue
        if not block:
            return message
    return message
```
After timeout, the internal consumer returns None, and the loop continues.

issue_title: Return a Mask class from the to_mask() method
issue_body: Just to follow-up on https://github.com/astropy/regions/issues/30, it would be better if the `to_mask()` method returns a `Mask` object.  The `Mask` object would be initialized with the mask array and the tuple slice and it could then handle the cases where the aperture is partially (or completely) outside of the image.

issue_title: build_docs swallows errors b/c of changed code in Sphinx
issue_body:
When there is an error in the Sphinx build, fails in astropy-helpers' build_docs command fails with 
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 39, in <module>
NameError: name 'self' is not defined
Sphinx Documentation subprocess failed with return code 1
```

This is because astropy injects the run method of build_docs into a subprocess, but doesn't inject the command object itself. The command object (``self``) is referenced in [line 190 of sphinx/setup_command.py](https://github.com/sphinx-doc/sphinx/blob/master/sphinx/setup_command.py#L190). The code generated for the subprocess is

```
from __future__ import print_function
from sphinx.setup_command import *

os.chdir(u'/Users/alexrudy/Dropbox/Library/Development/Astronomy/Zeeko/docs')
sys.path.insert(0, u'/Users/alexrudy/Dropbox/Library/Development/Astronomy/Zeeko/build/lib.macosx-10.11-x86_64-2.7')
sys.path.insert(0, '/Users/alexrudy/Dropbox/Library/Development/Astronomy/Zeeko/astropy_helpers')

if not color_terminal():
    nocolor()
if not 3:
    status_stream = StringIO()
else:
    status_stream = sys.stdout
confoverrides = {}
if '':
    confoverrides['project'] = ''
if '':
    confoverrides['version'] = ''
if '':
    confoverrides['release'] = ''
if '':
    confoverrides['today'] = ''
if '':
    confoverrides['copyright'] = ''

app = None
try:
    with docutils_namespace():
        app = Sphinx(u'./docs', u'./docs',
                     u'./build/sphinx/html', u'./build/sphinx/doctrees',
                     'html', confoverrides, status_stream,
                     freshenv=False,
                     warningiserror=False)
        app.build(force_all=False)
        if app.statuscode:
            raise DistutilsExecError(
                'caused by %s builder.' % app.builder.name)
except Exception as exc:
    handle_exception(app, self, exc, sys.stderr)
    if not False:
        raise SystemExit(1)

if False:
    src = app.config.master_doc + app.builder.out_suffix
    dst = app.builder.get_outfilename('index')
    os.symlink(src, dst)

```

I'm not sure what to do about this. Maybe pickle the command object? That seems bad, and swallowing the error is really frustrating.

issue_title: Retry mechanism for failing requests
issue_body:
Whether stripe times out, there are other network issues or general randomness of the interwebz it's good to be able to retry. 

So I would suggest that you implement a retry mechanism as described here: https://gist.github.com/benjiao/28dc36bd87121b3273e0b3e079a8e8d8

Make it an optional parameter for when initializing the client with a sane default. That way the user of the library can customize it if they deem it necessary.

issue_title: Urgent feature request: Dynamically set qadapter variables
issue_body:
There are certain types of jobs where the number of nodes need to vary based on the actual calculation. A prime example is NEB, where number of nodes is equal to number of images usually. 

Right now, the FW # of nodes is hard-coded in the qadapter.yaml. While we can create multiple qadapters to cater to different number of node runs, this is extremely cumbersome to manage.

We need a way for qlaunch to dynamically set certain variables based on job type.

Note that this applies only for non-reservation mode, where it is one specific job per launch. 

@HanmeiTang pls provide more info as needed.

issue_title: SBP object implementation can't use slots
issue_body:
http://bugs.python.org/issue15535

As far as I can tell, if you implement a class with slots, the python pickling library will have difficulties, looking for an implicit `__dict__` attribute when none exists. Incidentally, you can add a `__dict__` attribute try and fool python, but then it'll go ahead and call `dict()` anyway (which sucks). In any case, whenever we've moved off using picking serialization for anything, we ought to use `__slots__` for the memory help, since it may actually be useful here for reducing memory usage of serial_link and the testing stuff.

For an implementation example, see: https://github.com/construct/construct/blob/master/construct/core.py#L100

I'm trying to create a dynamic list of authors, where each author has a dynamic list of affiliations, where each affiliation has a few subfields.

I tried the following code, but the nested lists won't work in the browser.

        default='',

--------------------------------------------------from kivy.app import App
from kivy.uix.widget import Widget
from kivy.properties import NumericProperty, ReferenceListProperty, ObjectProperty
from kivy.vector import Vector
from kivy.clock import Clock

class PongPaddle(Widget):
    score = NumericProperty(0)

    def bounce_ball(self, ball):
        if self.collide_widget(ball):
            vx, vy = ball.velocity
            offeset = (ball.center_y - self.center_y) / (self.height / 2)
            bounced = Vector(-1 * vx, vy)
            vel = bounced * 1.1
            ball.velocity = vel.x, vel.y + offeset

class PongBall(Widget):
    velocity_x = NumericProperty(0)
    velocity_y = NumericProperty(0)
    velocity = ReferenceListProperty(velocity_x, velocity_y)

    def move(self):
        self.pos = Vector(*self.velocity) + self.pos

class PongGame(Widget):
    ball = ObjectProperty(None)
    player1 = ObjectProperty(None)
    player2 = ObjectProperty(None)

    def serve_ball(self, vel=(4,0)):
        self.ball.center = self.center
        self.ball.velocity = vel

    def update(self, dt):
        self.ball.move()

        self.player1.bounce_ball(self.ball)
        self.player2.bounce_ball(self.ball)

        if(self.ball.y < self.y) or (self.ball.top > self.top):
            self.ball.velocity_y *= -1

        if self.ball.x < self.x:
            self.player2.score += 1
            self.serve_ball(vel=(4,0))

        if self.ball.x > self.width:
            self.player1.score +=1
            self.serve_ball(vel=(4,0))

    def on_touch_move(self, touch):
        if touch.x < self.width / 3:
            self.player1.center_y = touch.y

        if touch.x > self.width / 3:
            self.player2.center_y = touch.y



class PongApp(App):
    def build(self):
        game = PongGame()
        game.serve_ball()
        Clock.schedule_interval(game.update, 1.0 / 60.0)

if __name__== '__main__':
    PongApp().run()
main.py

issue_title: Confusing / Outdated Bootstraps
issue_body:
I am trying to create a new bootstrap for a webview since the old one does not work anymore. So I copied the sdl2 gradle bootstrap. I adjusted it a bit and removed some lines of code. It's almost working except the fact that for some reason I can't figure out, this error happens: 
```
java.lang.UnsatisfiedLinkError: No implementation found for int org.kivy.android.PythonActivity.nativeInit(java.lang.Object) (tried Java_org_kivy_android_PythonActivity_nativeInit and Java_org_kivy_android_PythonActivity_nativeInit__Ljava_lang_Object_2)

```

I am not an expert but it seems to me that a nativeInit implementation in `C` language is missing. However I none of the bootstraps (including the original sdl2 bootstrap) I found a declaration of `nativeInit` in C. Where do I find the nativeInit C function? Where should it be declared? Why could it be excluded? Logcat says that it loaded any libraries (main, sdl, python2.7) except python3.5m (since I did not compile with Python 3) -> so this looks fine. However a C function for nativeInit seems to be missing.

issue_title: Operators not defined in Expression
issue_body:
In expressions.py, class ExpressionNode:

line 451:
    if sys.version_info[0] < 3:
        __div__ = div_op
        __rdiv__ = binop('div', reversed=True)

All fine, but the operators are not defined for Python 3. Leading to unexpected behavior in conjunction with for instance the floor division operator `//`.

issue_title: Custom dictionary causes failed round trip on some input
Separate the public headers
issue_body: IMO, it would be cleaner to move the public headers to a separate `include` directory, and/or give them less generic names like `brotli-decode.h` and `brotli-encode.h`, to streamline the use of the library.

issue_title: Use package name when __main__ is used
annotations and kwonlyargs
issue_body:
inspectutils.GetArgSpec uses inspect.getargspec, which can't handle annotations and kwonly args. The program

```
from fire import Fire

def test_args(arg1: str = 'aarrrgh', *, arg2: str = 'arrrgh'):
    return arg2, arg2

if __name__ == '__main__':
    Fire(test_args)
```
leads to an error at line 75 in GetArgSpec
"ValueError: Function has keyword-only parameters or annotations, use getfullargspec() API which can support them"

The simplest fix would be to put in another except clause to catch the value error, and proceed with inspect.getfullargspec or inspect.signature instead. An alternate way might be to decide at import if the program is python 3 and inspect.signature can be called. Do you have a preference between the two solutions, or another suggestion?

-Jared

issue_title: Clarify integration with Celery when using Django
issue_body:
Hello,

I am a bit confused by the documentation on how to use Raven with Celery in the context of Django.

The [documentation](https://docs.sentry.io/clients/python/integrations/django/) states that « If you are also using Celery, there is a handler being automatically registered for you that captures the errors from workers. ». Which is quite cool!

However I have noticed that Celery-based errors come to Sentry with very little information − no stack-trace, no breadcrumbs.

Conversely, I have another Django app where (mainly for historical reasons) we registered Raven both in the Django configuration and using the [Celery integration instructions](https://sentry.io/for/celery/) − and the error reporting in Sentry is better. (But it might be for other reasons / configurations)

My question would be : is the handler automatically set via Django expected to be a fully-fledged integration, or is it a basic integration, and it is needed to also use the `register_signal` snippets to?

The docs could also use a short sentence making that clearer :)

Thanks !

issue_title: Icon request: fa-starts-with, fa-contains, fa-ends-with
issue_body:
For use in a form filter where user can select predicate, or to indicate a fixed predicate in a form filter

Example usage:

![immagine](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3042285/43320805-0963da9c-91aa-11e8-9249-5dbe19492d34.png)

# Icons requested

## fa-starts-with
Indicates a value (usually a name, a string) "starting with"

Proposed design: `[...`

## fa-ends-with

Opposite to the above. Proposed design: `...]`

## fa-contains

A mix of the two above. An icon indicating that the value is contained
Proposed design: `[...]`

# Further development
Other ideas for additional icons

You may want to distinguish between inclusive and exclusive (AFAIK this is indicated with square `[]` and round `()` braces).
You may want to consider a variant using curly `{}` braces, or diamond `<>` braces

issue_title: Erroneous Error on Exec Depends That are Also Test Depends
issue_body:
I have found that if I find_package() a dependency for unit tests (in my case, roslaunch), catkin_lint will find an error unless the dependency is listed as a test_depend or a build_depend. Listing the package as an exec_depend does not fulfill the requirement for catkin_lint.

According to http://wiki.ros.org/catkin/package.xml, "Test Dependencies specify only additional dependencies for unit tests. They should never duplicate any dependencies already mentioned as build or run dependencies. " It seems that specifying the package as an exec_depend fulfills this requirement, so catkin_lint shouldn't consider this an error.

issue_title: Better model saving/loading
issue_body: Currently, cnn models use model the boost serialization library to perform model saving/loading. This is easy, but only works if you use the same version of boost to save/load files. Ideally, we can create a version of model saving that works across boost versions, perhaps by creating a custom boost serializer that reads/writes the parameters appropriately in text format.

issue_title: Logging: expose "uniqueWriterIdentity" for creating sinks
issue_body:
Derived from SO question [here](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/47769820/how-to-set-uniquewriteridentity-true-on-sink-create).

To create sinks cross-project, `uniqueWriterIdentity` (see [here](https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/reference/v2/rest/v2/projects.sinks/create)) needs to be set to `true`, otherwise the following error is thrown:

`google.gax.errors.RetryError: RetryError(Exception occurred in retry method that was not classified as transient, caused by <_Rendezvous of RPC that terminated with (StatusCode.INVALID_ARGUMENT, The destination project must be the same as the sink's project. Use uniqueWriterIdentity=true to allow cross project exports.)>)`

Currently this is not supported by the SDK. It should be.

Until it's supported, it should also be clearly documented that it's not possible to use this SDK to create sinks cross-project.

issue_title: `TaskBase#check_artifact_cache_for` and `TaskBase#maybe_write_artifact` do not pair well.
issue_body: The semi-automatic caching supported by `TaskBase` via an override of `TaskBase#cache_target_dirs` does not work when paired with an override of `TaskBase#check_artifact_cache_for`. IvyResolve demonstrates this by overriding `TaskBase#check_artifact_cache_for` and not `TaskBase#cache_target_dirs`; instead implementing completely custom cache updates. Either the docs for `TaskBase#check_artifact_cache_for` should be made explicit wrt to breaking semi-automatic artifact caching or else the semi-automatic artifact caching should be fixed to cache for the same VTS list returned by `TaskBase#check_artifact_cache_for`.

issue_title: Lambda trigger always points to Prod stage
issue_body:
When creating a new API Gateway API by using the implicit functionality of `AWS::Serverless::Function` two stages `Prod` and `Stage` get created. As mentioned in #78, we shouldn't care about `Stage`, so let's have a look at `Prod` only, because if you create an API explicitly via `AWS::Serverless::Api` with a different name than `Prod`, everything gets deployed successful and works as expected, but the trigger for the Lambda functions still refer to a (not existing) `Prod` stage.
Is that a bug or intentional?

issue_title: Policy create issues with Dead Letter Queue and XRay Tracing
issue_body:
I tried adding my function Dead Letter Queue(DLQ) SQS queue and enabling XRay tracing. The stack executer role also had the permissions enabled for the following resources `sqs:*` and `xray:*`.

The function has following inline policies for XRay:
```
- Effect: "Allow" # xray permissions (required)
  Action:
    - "xray:PutTraceSegments"
    - "xray:PutTelemetryRecords"
  Resource:
    - "*"
```

For SQS:
```
- Effect: Allow
  Action:
    - sqs:SendMessage
  Resource: "*"
```

When the stack's changeset is applied it would fail because:
```

12:47:11 UTC+0200 | UPDATE_ROLLBACK_IN_PROGRESS | AWS::CloudFormation::Stack | notifications-lambda | The following resource(s) failed to update: [GeneratorFunction].
-- | -- | -- | -- | --
  | 12:47:08 UTC+0200 | UPDATE_FAILED | AWS::Lambda::Function | GeneratorFunction | The provided execution role does not have permissions to call PutTraceSegments on XRAY
```

The current workaround is to deploy the function first time without the following options:
```
      DeadLetterQueue:
        Type: SQS
        TargetArn: !GetAtt NotificationDeadLetterQueue.Arn
      Tracing: Active
```
After that you can enable the options again, deploy and the stack apply would succeed. Seems like the serverless guys have same issue https://github.com/serverless/serverless/pull/3742.

issue_title: Incorrect IP to stdout on startup
issue_body:
## Summary

Kolibri indicates the server is listening on 0.0.0.0:8080 on startup. This is not RFC 1122 compliant and is causing confusion to OpCos. Please can this be changed to the IP (or IPs):8080?

## System information

*Please specify the Kolibri version you were using and your operating system!*

 - Version: kolibri-vf-v0.3.1
 - Operating system: Ubuntu
 - Browser: N/A

## Traceback or relevant snippet from server.log or browser console

![wrong_loopback](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/7903978/26819494/03fdd946-4a97-11e7-91c8-85298e931bee.jpg)


## How to reproduce

1. Start Kolibri
1. Read the resulting stdout 'Engine serving on...' message

## Real-life consequences (anything community should be aware of, for instance how it affects your deployment)

Confusion from network admins who cannot access that IP - per RFC 1122, 'This host on this network.  MUST NOT be sent, except as a source address as part of an initialization procedure by which the host learns its own IP address.' So any attenpt to access via a browser or other box will fail, as both proxys and routers will drop packets addressed this way.

After submitting the exam the learner is still allowed to start the exam. Where as it should show the **Completed** and the learner should not be allowed to retake.

issue_title: Human Readable CSV Findings Export
issue_body:
Hi,

Although various export are available and the html/js report offers an impressive user experience, a lack of easy to use, human readable csv export of scout2 findings is perhaps the one thing that keeps it from being, hands down, the best aws audit tool.

I may be missing something, even though i've spent many hours on the issue throwing myself at the [ export wiki entry ](https://github.com/nccgroup/Scout2/wiki/HowTo:-Exporting-and-Programmatically-Access-of-Scout2-Data). 
Or perhaps it was never the intention to make scout2 easy to use for mere mortals.

Regardless, i'd love to walk out of this experience a bit wiser.
Enlighten me, would you kindly :) 
-A.G.

issue_title: docs/installation process updates
issue_body:
In the docs (README.rst) we have:
```
Run migrations: python manage.py migrate aldryn_newsblog.

NOTE: aldryn_newsblog supports both South and Django 1.7 migrations. If using Django 1.7, you may need to add the following to your settings:

MIGRATION_MODULES = [
   …
   'aldryn_newsblog': 'aldryn_newsblog.south_migrations',
   # The following are for some of the depenencies.
   'djangocms_text_ckeditor': 'djangocms_text_ckeditor.migrations_django',
   'filer': 'filer.migrations_django',
   …
]
```
It should be something like this:
```py
MIGRATION_MODULES = {
   …
   # The following are for some of the dependencies.
   'djangocms_text_ckeditor': 'djangocms_text_ckeditor.migrations_django',
   'filer': 'filer.migrations_django',
   …
}
```
* {} instead of []
* aldryn_newsblog in 'dev' branch uses django migrations by default;
* dependencies typo fix

issue_title: Check for compatibility with redirect responses from the API
issue_body:
Sometimes, resources move: things are renamed or reorganized, or deleted and re-created. An API should be able to issue [redirect responses](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_HTTP_status_codes#3xx_Redirection) to clients seeking information about resources that have moved.

I don't know if this SDK works properly with redirected responses. By default, the `requests` library [will handle redirects for you](http://docs.python-requests.org/en/master/user/quickstart/#redirection-and-history), but we should verify that it does the right thing.

issue_title: Methods download() and install() in SoftwareUpdater only perform action if sync=True.
issue_body:
I've been writing some code for Ansible modules, and I've noticed the download and install methods in SoftwareUpdater only perform the action if the sync parameter is set to true:

(Install method displayed, download is similar.)
```
    def install(self, version, load_config=None, sync=False):
        self._logger.info("Device %s installing version: %s" % (self.pandevice.id, version))
        response = self._op('request system software install%s version "%s"' %
                            (" load-config " + load_config if load_config is not None else "",
                             version))
        if sync:
            result = self.pandevice.syncjob(response)
            if not result['success']:
                raise err.PanDeviceError("Device %s attempt to install version %s failed: %s" %
                                         (self.pandevice.id, version, result['messages']))
            return result
        else:
            return True
```

Is this intentional?

issue_title: PayInPaymentDetailsDirectCard inherits the wrong class
issue_body:
I think PayInPaymentDetailsDirectCard inherits the wrong class, see https://github.com/Mangopay/mangopay2-python-sdk/blob/master/mangopaysdk/types/payinpaymentdetailsdirectcard.py

Shouldn't it inherit from `mangopaysdk.types.payinpaymentdetails.PayInPaymentDetails` like the other payment details classes?

issue_title: Client with null kwargs does not use default parameters
issue_body:
Currently jwplatform.Client instantiation only uses default parameters if a kwarg doesn't exist. If the kwarg is `None` this value is still used. It would be expected that `None` values for a kwarg use the default value.

**Current**
```python
>>> import jwplatform
>>> client = jwplatform.Client('key', 'secret', host=None)
>>> client._host == 'api.jwplatform.com'
False
```

**Expected**
```python
>>> import jwplatform
>>> client = jwplatform.Client('key', 'secret', host=None)
>>> client._host == 'api.jwplatform.com'
True
```

issue_title: `site_cfg` is not defined
issue_body:
When I tried to use `CPATH` environment variable to pass include dierctory, I got this:

```
  File "generate.py", line 99, in create_module
    site_cfg['include_dirs'] += [os.path.join(p, 'nanomsg')
NameError: global name 'site_cfg' is not defined
```

The reason is [this](https://github.com/nanomsg/nnpy/blob/master/generate.py#L99) line. It should probably be `set_source_args` instead of `site_cfg`

issue_title: Is it possible to allow requests 2.5.2+
issue_body:
I am aware of the issue with boot2docker and the assert_hostname thing, but it appears that the requests library made a change as of version 2.5.2 related to assert_hostname.  See:

http://docs.python-requests.org/en/latest/community/updates/#id2
https://github.com/shazow/urllib3/pull/526

I am not a Mac user and don't use boot2docker, but I was wondering if this change to 'requests' would allow docker-py to start using the newer versions of the library.  It would be nice if my system didn't have to stay with requests 2.4.3 just to support docker-py.

issue_title: support 'synchronous' code with a fake loop
issue_body:
add support for a mode where every Future completes synchronously.

This way someone could write an adapter that can use one of twisted.web, requests or aiohttp and still get back a future.

Except if you used requests you could synchronously unwrap any futures.

issue_title: Proposal: Change format of `/v1/apps/<app id>/run`
issue_body:
Currently run returns a dynamic array (Ewww!) `[0, "hi\n"]`. The docs said that run returned `{"rc": 0, "output": "hi"}` which seems much more sensible to me.

I would like propose to change the response of run to the format suggested to the docs:

```json
{
  "rc": <return code>, 
  "output": "<command output>"
}

```

This is a backwards incompatible change, so this would probably warrant a API version bump to version 2.

The good news is that for [as long as we have had API docs](http://docs.deis.io/en/latest/reference/api-v1.0/#run-one-off-commands) nobody has caught this huge difference, so it seems unlikely that many people are consuming that part of the api.

issue_title: Logspout only discovers logger at startup
issue_body:
Logspout only discovers the host and port for the `deis-logger` at startup.  If `deis-logger` is rescheduled to another machine, logs are no longer aggregated.

For other components that have a dependency on another component that could move around the cluster, we rely on confd to reconfigure the dependent component.  Since our logspout is a forked and _minimally_ modified version of an older version of logspout, we're not doing any of this.

This also relates to #4292

issue_title: Proposal: containerize the development environment
issue_body:
Currently to hack on, build, or test _all_ of Deis, there's no shortage of things that need to be installed into the _local_ test environment.  This includes Docker, Go, Python, PostgreSQL, shellcheck and more.  This can be daunting to set up for a new contributor.  Even seasoned contributors can have some difficulty transitioning to a new machine or after upgrading their OS.

What about containerizing the whole shooting match?  This could lower the barrier to just Docker and make.

issue_title: confd: Limit scope of etcd --watch to /deis or remove it altogether
issue_body:
See coreos/etcd#2679. confd is refreshing keys whenever their TTLs change, which is quite frequently, likely due to:

- Watching the entire etcd keyspace rather than just `/deis`
- Each component's published keys (host/port) 

The etcd team is discussing adding support to filter out TTL updates from "normal" key changes, but in the meantime we need to investigate whether limiting watch to `/deis` reduces traffic substantially. If it doesn't, we'll need to disable watch and revert to a 5s polling interval. 

refs #3501
refs coreos/etcd#2679

issue_title: Option to override numpy dtype for decimals
issue_body: I'm pulling some numbers that are represented as 'length 38 numeric' type in SQL Server (even though the size is overkill for the actual data), and turbodbc is converting them to strings (as stated [here](http://turbodbc.readthedocs.io/en/latest/pages/advanced_usage.html#notes-regarding-numpy-support)). Would it be possible to allow the user to specify that these be returned as floats?

issue_title: Implicit pybind11 dependency
issue_body: When you try the compilation from source and you haven't installed `pybind11` then `setup.py` raises the error message `ImportError: No module named pybind11`, so the `pybind11` dependency seems to be missing in the explicit setup dependencies.

issue_title: Creating a filter generates duplicate events in other filters
issue_body:
* Version: 0.1.0b27
* Python: 3.5
* OS: linux


### What was wrong?

When creating a new filter using `create_log_filter()`, `_process_blocks_logs()` is called to populate filter with past events.
This method however calls `add()` method for all existing filters:
```python
    def _process_block_logs(self, block):
        for _, filter in self._log_filters.items():
            for transaction_hash in block['transactions']:
                receipt = self.get_transaction_receipt(transaction_hash)
                for log_entry in receipt['logs']:
                    raw_log_entry = self.normalizer.normalize_inbound_log_entry(log_entry)
                    filter.add(raw_log_entry)
```



### How can it be fixed?

`Filter::add()` method should check if the log entry already exists in `Filter::values` before appending/processing it.

issue_title: Make CTRL-C in the middle of conda update operations robust
support for package containers (eg. wheel)
issue_body:
If one installs conda to a network filesystem, imports take a long time, because the installation method of conda enforces unpacking everything. This problem has already been solved by the wheel and egg zip file formats, which avoid the overhead of reading thousands of individual files. The Java world has jars for this purpose. So I got some questions.
* Is there any good reason to enforce unpacking wheels in the first place?
* Is wheel interfering with the meta-data concept of conda in any way? Duplicating the info wouldn't be a problem, would it?
* Do you think it would be hard to implement? (eg. just omit the unzip step and just copy the archive file to site-packages)

Thanks in advance for your feedback on this. I hope it is not a duplicate (githubs search feature is kinda limited, sorry).

issue_title: Change default value for 'allow_softlinks' from True to False
issue_body: The `allow_softlinks` configuration parameter can be dangerous.  Softlinks into prefixes directly from the package cache will often lead to environment corruption.  It was an oversight that the default value for `allow_softlinks` is set to `True`.  In conda 4.4, we should definitely change the default value to `False`.  We should also consider it even for the next 4.3.x release.

issue_title: Installing tar
issue_body:
Hello,

According to: 

https://docs.continuum.io/anaconda/faq#how-do-i-install-packages-on-a-non-networked-machine

You can install a bundle of packages by first creating a tar, and then simply using ``conda install``. The bundle would just install them without doing any checking - which is indeed the desired behavior if you're assuming you created a consistent bundle.

I found out that this works nicely on conda 4.2.12, but not anymore on 4.3.17 (I didn't test versions in between).

Is there a reason this behavior has changed (and, certainly, the doc is out of sync with the latest behavior). 

What would be the preferred way to make a 'bundle' of packages? My use case is that we have a proper environment already set up, and I want to deploy additional packages in that environment (which have been tested to work and do not create dependency problems).

Thanks for looking into this and advising!

issue_title: Fetching from a CRAM file fails if multiple_iterators is False
issue_body:
The following fails for CRAM files, but works for BAM files:

``` python
        for a, b in zip(samfile1.fetch(contig, start, stop,
                                       multiple_iterators=False),
                        samfile1.fetch(contig, start, stop,
                                       multiple_iterators=True)):
            self.assertEqual(a.compare(b), 0)
```

while the following works in both cases:

``` python
        for a, b in zip(samfile1.fetch(contig, start, stop,
                                       multiple_iterators=False),
                        samfile1.fetch(contig, start, stop,
                                       multiple_iterators=True)):
            self.assertEqual(a.compare(b), 0)
```

See AlignmentFile_test.py testDoubleFetchCRAMWithRegion.

issue_title: Curator deletes index patterns and saved searches

issue_title: On Python 2, upgrading from 0.1.0b10 to 0.1.0b11 may cause 'KeyError: Azure' error
issue_body:
On Python 2, it's recommended to upgrade with the `--ignore-installed` flag:
`pip install --upgrade --ignore-installed azure-cli`.

If the above isn't followed, KeyError may appear as pip fails to install 2 required files.
Add these manually with:
```
touch <path_to_site_packages>/azure/cli/__init__.py
touch <path_to_site_packages>/azure/cli/command_modules/__init__.py
```

<path_to_site_packages> is the path to the environment the CLI was installed into.
For example /usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages.

This is a one-time change.

Fresh installs are not affected.

---
More info:

Upgrading from the previous version of the CLI to this version breaks on Python 2.
It breaks because pip attempts to uninstall the old version first so https://github.com/Azure/azure-cli/issues/1406 occurs again.
Python 3 works because of implicit namespace package support.

issue_title: generate-sas generate useless SAS if permission and expiry are not given.
issue_body:

Recommendation, have default permission or expiry set to the token.

Could you please take a look if this is a bug or simply I just miss something? Thanks

issue_title: Low BLOB_MAX_CHUNK_DATA_SIZE leads to performance hits for max_connections=1
issue_body:
### Description:
When using azure.storage.blobservice.get_blob_to_file, the algorithm will download a 64MB (or smaller) file in one call REST call.

However larger files are chunked in 4MB blocks.  For a 65MB file this would result in 17 calls, which significantly decreases performance.  For my application, changing _BLOB_MAX_CHUNK_DATA_SIZE to be the same as _BLOB_MAX_DATA_SIZE (64MB) improved performance drastically.

Not sure of all use cases to know if this will impact other applications, but if it cannot be hard coded to 64MB, perhaps allowing a chunk_size  argument would help.

##### Original Issue: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-python/issues/337
### Comments (1):
#### @huguesv commented on 2015-04-14 22:33:03:
> Thanks for letting us know about this.  Looking at the node storage sdk, it appears that download chunk size may not be limited to 4MB, unless the md5 is requested.  I'll do some testing.
